## [00:00 - 03:00]

Most people are unwilling to deal with the emotional discomfort that takes place between the no and the yes, and actually seek to avoid this emotional exchange at almost all costs. This retreat and desire to avoid emotional output is a retreat from life itself and definitely a retreat from success. You look, you have to be willing to handle anything and everything, including the emotional output from others in order to be with them and accomplish anything in life. Okay, what I'm talking about is not pulling back when somebody gets like, you know, ferocious or loud or noisy or angry or, you know, I want to leave. When it comes to negotiations, agreements, decisions and money, the close people get emotional and most of it is only an emotional response that has actually nothing to do with you or even your proposal. But you have to be trained to make nothing of it, right? Some guys getting emotional in my face. I got to get out of here. I've been here too long. This is ridiculous. I expected it to be. Look, you got to be, you got to be in a position to make nothing of it. Acknowledge your prospect. I understand. I know how you feel and avoid, avoid becoming emotional as well. Look, people get funny when it comes to making decisions. You ever notice yourself when it comes to making a decision? You get a little weird and especially weird when it comes to money. So you need to train yourself, literally drill handling people logically, not emotionally. This is a myth in selling. In the close, you do not want to be emotional. You want to be logical. In the close, you want to be non-emotional. Let them be emotional. You don't want to be negatively impacted from their emotions, positive or negative. See, emotion comes from the word to move. If the buyer is non-emotional, they're not going to move. So emotions are good. Even anger is better than apathy. Without emotion, you will not get a movement to close. It's not bad to have a buyer that's emoting, but it is absolutely critical that you don't get all emotional. When your prospect starts emoting, he or she is simply expressing some emotion that should not be giving more value than it deserves. Look, instead of getting emotional too in the close, okay? Remain calm. You got screaming at you. Stay calm. Remain calm. Stay interested. Stay interested in them, even though they're upset, and continue negotiating until you get the close.
